original_abstract: Objective: to formulate the bases for quantum law as the law of the future, based on the study of quantum phenomena and features of quantum technologies determining the risks and challenges associated with the emergence of these technologies, as well as the analysis of legal regulation of quantum technologies in the Russian Federation and abroad.Methods: to carry out this research, the authors applied a complex of general scientific methods of systemic analysis and specific methods of engineering and legal sciences. The use of comparative-legal method allowed revealing the main directions of developing legal regulation of quantum technologies in the Russian and foreign law orders based on the analysis of their international and national regulation. The method of legal modeling allowed forming a concept of quantum law, revealing the main vectors of its development and the complex of its ethical-legal principles.Results: the features and properties of quantum technologies were revealed which can change the development of law with the advent of these technologies; the main risks and challenges were identified which are associated with the development of quantum technologies; the features of quantum technologies regulation in some foreign countries were specified; the trends of developing the quantum technologies regulation in the Russian Federation were identified; the conceptual bases of quantum law were formulated, as well as the vectors of its development.Scientific novelty: for the first time in the legal science, a complex analysis of the current national (both Russian and foreign) regulation of quantum technologies was carried out, based on which an attempt was made to substantiate the need to form quantum law and to outline the main vectors of its development.Practical significance: the research results lay the foundation for forming the concept of quantum law; in this regard, the authors’ conclusions and proposals for improving the current regulation of quantum technologies can be used in law-making and law enforcement in this sphere, and may lay the bases for further research in the sphere of quantum technologies.
humanized_abstract: The purpose of this study is to formulate the bases of quantum law as a law of the future through research of quantum phenomena and features of quantum technologies that may pose a threat, risks and challenges to the emergence of quantum technologies as well as the legal regulation of quantum technologies in Russia and abroad. For this purpose, the authors have applied a whole range of general scientific methods of the systemic approach as well as special methods of engineering and legal sciences. In particular, the comparative-legal method made it possible to characterise the main trends of the regulation of quantum technologies in the domestic and foreign legal frameworks by examining the international and national legislation in this field. The legal modelling method enabled us to formulate the concept of quantum law, and identify the main trends of its development and a set of ethical and legal principles. As a result, the study has identified the features of quantum technologies that could affect the development of law with the emergence of quantum technologies; the main risks and challenges related to quantum technologies; characterised the regulation of quantum technologies in a number of foreign countries and identified trends in regulating quantum technologies in Russia. Conclusion: the study formulated the conceptual bases of quantum law and outlined the vectors of its development. The study has scientific novelty and practical significance. The scientific novelty of the study is that, for the first time in the legal literature, a comprehensive analysis of current domestic (Russian and foreign) regulation of quantum technologies was carried out and an attempt was made to prove the need to establish quantum law and to identify its main trends of development. The practical significance of the study lies in that the conclusions and recommendations of the authors for improving the current regulation of quantum technologies may be used in the law-making and law-enforcement activities in this sphere, and in that they can lay the foundation for further scientific research in the sphere of quantum technologies.
